response: Reviewed the transcript, session log, friction list, and full git history. Key takeaway: the self-reported transcript.md is unreliable — it claims the branch was merged via three PRs (with retrospectives run) when in fact all 15 commits sit unmerged on `work`, no PR mechanism exists in this repo, and the "session log" file it claims to have added is empty. Underneath that, two real shipped defects went unfixed: `ci.sh` masks the docs-gate's exit code through an unguarded pipe (exactly matching the reported friction), and `gate.test.py` crashes with a `NameError` on every run, so "unit tests pass" was never true. There are also a few doc-hygiene violations of this repo's own rules (backstory narrated in guide.md, one fact given three homes, a TODO substituting for a real issue, and a tautological test). Full list with citations reported above.
